steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both texts together support about the two clades. Text 1 defines Gondwanan clades as clades that predate Grande Terre's split from Gondwana approximately 80 million years ago. Text 2 gives crown ages: harvest spiders at 48.9 million years and cockroaches at 2.7 million years. Both crown ages are younger than the split.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The clade of harvest spiders must have originated on Gondwana, but the clade of cockroaches may or may not have.": Harvest spiders' crown age of 48.9 million years is younger than the 80-million-year split, so the clade does not predate the split as Text 1 requires for Gondwanan clades. ✗
  • "Both the clade of harvest spiders and the clade of cockroaches originated elsewhere and colonized Grande Terre.": The texts support that neither clade predates the split, but they do not establish the specific later colonization story for both clades. ✗
  • "Neither the clade of harvest spiders nor the clade of cockroaches originated on Gondwana.": Both crown ages, 48.9 million years and 2.7 million years, are younger than the 80-million-year split, so neither clade predates the split from Gondwana. ✓
  • "The clade of harvest spiders originated before Grande Terre split from Gondwana, whereas the clade of cockroaches originated after the split but before the periodic submergence events began.": Harvest spiders' crown age is after the split, and cockroaches' crown age is after the submergence period ended. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
